Effect version: 3.19.19

/.src/tryCatchInEffectGen.ts(5,3): suggestion TS377012: This Effect generator contains `try/catch`; in this context, error handling is expressed with Effect APIs such as Effect.try, Effect.tryPromise, Effect.catch, Effect.catchTag). effect(tryCatchInEffectGen)
/.src/tryCatchInEffectGen.ts(15,3): suggestion TS377012: This Effect generator contains `try/catch`; in this context, error handling is expressed with Effect APIs such as Effect.try, Effect.tryPromise, Effect.catch, Effect.catchTag). effect(tryCatchInEffectGen)
/.src/tryCatchInEffectGen.ts(40,5): suggestion TS377012: This Effect generator contains `try/catch`; in this context, error handling is expressed with Effect APIs such as Effect.try, Effect.tryPromise, Effect.catch, Effect.catchTag). effect(tryCatchInEffectGen)


==== /.src/tryCatchInEffectGen.ts (3 errors) ====
    // @effect-v3
    import * as Effect from "effect/Effect"
    
    export const shouldTrigger = Effect.gen(function*() {
      try {
      ~~~~~
        const result = yield* Effect.succeed(42)
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
        return result
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      } catch (error) {
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
        console.error(error)
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      }
    ~~~
!!! suggestion TS377012: This Effect generator contains `try/catch`; in this context, error handling is expressed with Effect APIs such as Effect.try, Effect.tryPromise, Effect.catch, Effect.catchTag). effect(tryCatchInEffectGen)
    })
    
    // This should also trigger with Effect.fn
    export const exampleWithEffectFn = Effect.fn("example")(function*() {
      try {
      ~~~~~
        yield* Effect.succeed("hello")
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      } catch (e) {
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
        console.error(e)
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
      }
    ~~~
!!! suggestion TS377012: This Effect generator contains `try/catch`; in this context, error handling is expressed with Effect APIs such as Effect.try, Effect.tryPromise, Effect.catch, Effect.catchTag). effect(tryCatchInEffectGen)
    })
    
    // This should NOT trigger (no try/catch)
    export const shouldNotTrigger = Effect.gen(function*() {
      const result = yield* Effect.succeed(42)
      return result
    })
    
    // This should NOT trigger (try/catch outside Effect.gen)
    export function regularFunction() {
      try {
        console.log("regular try/catch")
      } catch (e) {
        console.error(e)
      }
    }
    
    // Nested case should trigger
    export const nestedExample = Effect.gen(function*() {
      const inner = yield* Effect.gen(function*() {
        try {
        ~~~~~
          return yield* Effect.succeed(1)
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
        } catch (e) {
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
          console.error(e)
    ~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~~
        }
    ~~~~~
!!! suggestion TS377012: This Effect generator contains `try/catch`; in this context, error handling is expressed with Effect APIs such as Effect.try, Effect.tryPromise, Effect.catch, Effect.catchTag). effect(tryCatchInEffectGen)
      })
      return inner
    })
